title: Absconding to avoid service of summons or other proceeding
chapter_title: Of Contempts of the Lawful Authority of Public Servants
content: Whoever absconds in order to avoid being served with a summons, notice or order proceeding from any public servant legally empowered as such public servant to issue the same, shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to one thousand rupees, or with both.

footnotes.text: Absconding means hiding or fleeing your usual place of residence to intentionally frustrate judicial process. It is a specific intent crime.

title: Obstructing public servant in discharge of public functions
chapter_title: Of Contempts of the Lawful Authority of Public Servants
content: Whoever voluntarily obstructs any public servant in the discharge of his public functions, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three months, or with fine which may extend to fifteen hundred rupees, or with both.

footnotes.text: Requires an active physical impediment or threat. Mere verbal argument, refusal to answer questions, or passive non-cooperation typically does not satisfy the high threshold of physical 'obstruction'.
